error_message: Payment gateway timeout on Stripe API
root_cause: Stripe API rate limit exceeded due to sudden spike in retry requests
fix_applied: Implemented exponential backoff in retry logic and increased circuit breaker timeout to 15s

root_cause: Connection leak in the newly deployed reporting service holding connections open
fix_applied: Increased DB connection pool size from 10 to 20 and hotfixed the reporting service to close connections

root_cause: Backend auth-api pods were OOM killed due to memory leak
fix_applied: Restarted auth-api pods and increased memory limits from 512Mi to 1Gi

root_cause: Expired public key rotation on the identity provider side
fix_applied: Manually synced the new public keys to the auth cache and restarted the service

root_cause: Large KEYS * command run by a misconfigured cron job blocked the single thread
fix_applied: Killed the cron job, changed to use SCAN instead of KEYS in the script

root_cause: DDoS attack overwhelming the ingress controller memory buffers
fix_applied: Enabled Cloudflare under-attack mode and added rate limiting to the ingress config

root_cause: WAL logs were not being archived due to failed S3 backup script
fix_applied: Fixed AWS permissions on the IAM role for backups, triggered manual WAL archive, and resized EBS volume temporarily

root_cause: A newly deployed frontend version used an old environment variable for the Stripe key
fix_applied: Rolled back frontend deployment to previous version and updated CI/CD secrets

root_cause: Twilio account balance depleted
fix_applied: Added auto-recharge to the Twilio account with corporate card

error_message: High CPU usage on auth pod
root_cause: Bcrypt work factor was accidentally increased from 10 to 14 in the latest release
fix_applied: Reverted bcrypt work factor change in config map and performed rolling restart

error_message: Deadlock found when trying to get lock
root_cause: Concurrent bulk update processes conflicting on the same rows in items table
fix_applied: Added explicit ordering to row updates to prevent deadlocks and reduced batch size

root_cause: Unassigned replica shards due to a crashed node in zone b
fix_applied: Rebooted the crashed ES node and waited for shard reallocation

error_message: Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined
root_cause: API response payload changed without versioning, removing a required field
fix_applied: Added null checks in the frontend component and reverted API change pending proper deprecation

root_cause: Complex SQL query taking too long on unindexed column
fix_applied: Added missing index on `created_at` column in the transactions table

root_cause: A traffic spike caused autoscaling to spawn too many app instances, exhausting max_connections
fix_applied: Increased max_connections temporarily and deployed PgBouncer as a connection pooler

root_cause: Temporary resized images were not being cleaned up from the local pod filesystem
fix_applied: Added a cleanup step after S3 upload and modified app to stream processing without saving to disk

root_cause: Upstream service was stuck in a restart loop due to failing readiness probe
fix_applied: Increased readiness probe initialDelaySeconds from 5s to 30s for the upstream service

root_cause: Default TTL on session keys was accidentally removed in recent commit
fix_applied: Restored the TTL setting on session cache writes and ran a script to clear untracked keys

root_cause: Payment provider rotated their webhook signing secrets
fix_applied: Updated the WEBHOOK_SECRET env var and restarted the payment service pods

root_cause: New admin dashboard domain was not added to the allowed origins list
fix_applied: Updated CORS configuration array to include the new domain and deployed
